title: Find Maximum in Array
description:
Given an integer array, find and return the maximum element.

Example:
Input: [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]
Output: 9

Constraints:
- Array can be empty
- Array can have negative numbers
- Array can have duplicate values

title: Sum of Array Elements
description:
Given an integer array, return the sum of all elements.

Example:
Input: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
Output: 15

Constraints:
- Array can be empty (return 0)
- Array contains both positive and negative integers

title: Reverse an Array
description:
Given an integer array, return the array reversed in-place.

Example:
Input: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
Output: [5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

Constraints:
- Must reverse in-place (no extra array)
- Array can be empty
- Array can have single element

title: Check if Array is Sorted
description:
Given an integer array, return true if it is sorted in non-decreasing order, false otherwise.

Example:
Input: [1, 2, 2, 3, 5]
Output: true

Input: [1, 3, 2]
Output: false

Constraints:
- Empty array is considered sorted
- Single element array is considered sorted

title: Find Duplicate in Array
description:
Given an integer array, return the first duplicate element found. If no duplicate exists, return -1.

Example:
Input: [1, 3, 4, 2, 2]
Output: 2

Constraints:
- Array can be empty
- Return -1 if no duplicate found

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an integer array and a target value, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target.

Example:
Input: nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9
Output: [0, 1]

Constraints:
- Exactly one solution exists
- Cannot use the same element twice
- Return indices in any order

title: Move Zeros to End
description:
Given an integer array, move all zeros to the end while maintaining the relative order of non-zero elements. Do this in-place.

Example:
Input: [0, 1, 0, 3, 12]
Output: [1, 3, 12, 0, 0]

Constraints:
- Must be in-place
- Maintain relative order of non-zero elements
- Array can be all zeros or no zeros

title: Maximum Subarray Sum
description:
Given an integer array, find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return that sum.

Example:
Input: [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
Output: 6 (subarray [4, -1, 2, 1])

Constraints:
- Array contains at least one element
- Array can contain all negative numbers

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description:
Given a sorted array, remove duplicates in-place and return the new length.

Example:
Input: [1, 1, 2, 3, 3]
Output: 3 (array becomes [1, 2, 3, ...])

Constraints:
- Must be in-place
- Order must be maintained
- Return the count of unique elements

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description:
Given an array where each element is the price of a stock on that day, return the maximum profit you can achieve. You may only complete one transaction.

Example:
Input: [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4]
Output: 5 (buy at 1, sell at 6)

Constraints:
- If no profit is possible return 0
- Cannot sell before buying
- Array can have all decreasing prices
